html.p: Welcome to the NetFlow Analytics for Splunk Application!
This App relies on *flow data processed by NetFlow Optimizer™ (NFO) and enables you to analyze it using Splunk® Enterprise or Splunk® Cloud.
NFO supports NetFlow v5, v9, sFlow, J-Flow, IPFIX, Cisco ASA NSEL, Cisco HSL, Cisco AVC, Palo Alto Networks NetFlow, Amazon VPC Flow Logs, Google VPC Flow Logs, Microsoft Azure NSG Flow Logs, etc.

ul.li: Visit https://www.netflowlogic.com/download/ to download NFO software.
Request a free 60 day NetFlow Optimizer trial license by completing the simple registration form
Configure NetFlow Optimizer input to receive NetFlow/sFlow/IPFIX, and output to send NFO syslogs to your Splunk system.

html.p: To learn more about NetFlow Optimizer please visit https://docs.netflowlogic.com/.
The App should be installed on your Splunk Search Heads.

This App requires the Technology Add-On for NetFlow (TA-netflow) (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1838/). Install this Add-on on your Splunk Search Head, Indexers, and Heavy Forwarders. To setup TA-netflow go to TA-netflow/setup_dashboard.
html.h3: 1. NETFLOW ANALYTICS FOR SPLUNK APP CONFIGURATION
html.p: The app can use data from events index and/or metrics index.
To use a different index from the default main or continue using a custom index for your NetFlow events, please perform the following on your searchheads:
In Settings->Advanced search->Search macros find the “netflow index” macro, click on it and change the value in the Definition field from: sourcetype=flowintegrator
to: index=flowintegrator sourcetype=flowintegrator in case the index where the flow data is stored is named flowintegrator
Alternatively, if you have SSH access to your Splunk servers, please perform the following:

ol.li:
Create the file if it does not already exist:
$SPLUNK_ROOT/etc/apps/netflow/local/macros.conf and add the following lines to it:
[netflow_index] definition = index=flowintegrator sourcetype=flowintegrator

html.p: We are assuming that the metrics index configured in the TA was named "netflow_metrics", if not change the index name in the following steps accordingly. In Settings->Advanced search->Search macros find the “netflow_metrics_index” macro, click on it and change the value in the Definition field to use the new index name: index=netflow_metrics sourcetype=netflow_metrics
Alternatively, if you have SSH access to your Splunk servers, please perform the following:

ol.li:
Create the file if it does not already exist:
$SPLUNK_ROOT/etc/apps/netflow/local/macros.conf and add the following lines to it:
[netflow_index] definition = index=netflow_metric sourcetype=netflow_metrics

html.p: 2. NOTE: Most dashboards have the following dropdwons: "NFO hostnames"/"Device group"/"Device (Exporter)". These dropdowns are populated automatically by saved searches which are running every 30 minutes. To refresh Device dropdown values press the "Update dropdowns" button.

panel.html: Click the button below to populate/update the dropdowns

html.p: Optionally, you can set up various lookup files for specific configurations. For details, visit https://docs.netflowlogic.com/integrations-and-apps/integrations-with-splunk/netflow-analytics-splunk-app/administration/#3-lookup-files-

If you ingest large volumes of NetFlow data, you may consider using summary indexes to improve search efficiency and application performance. For more information on saved searches included in this App, visit https://docs.netflowlogic.com/integrations-and-apps/integrations-with-splunk/netflow-analytics-splunk-app/summary-index-for-large-volumes-of-data
